title: Add paid amount and balance amount fields to invoices

requirements.text: Update the backend Motoko invoice data model to include two new fields: `paidAmount` (the amount paid so far, a numeric value) and `balanceAmount` (automatically calculated as total minus paidAmount). The `createInvoice` function should accept `paidAmount` as an optional input (defaulting to 0), and `balanceAmount` should be stored as `total - paidAmount`.

quotes: added paid amount and balance amount option

acceptanceCriteria: The invoice record in Motoko stores a `paidAmount` field (Nat or Float).
The invoice record stores a `balanceAmount` field computed as total minus paidAmount.
`createInvoice` accepts `paidAmount` (optional, defaults to 0).
Existing invoice retrieval functions return the new fields.
Balance amount is correctly calculated and persisted.

requirements.text: Update the Create Invoice page form to include a 'Paid Amount' input field. As the user types the paid amount, display a read-only 'Balance Due' field that auto-calculates as (total - paid amount). Both fields should appear in the totals section below the line items, styled consistently with the existing amber/teal theme.

quotes: added paid amount and balance amount option

acceptanceCriteria: A numeric 'Paid Amount' input is shown in the totals section of the Create Invoice form.
A read-only 'Balance Due' field is shown, updating reactively as subtotal or paid amount changes.
Balance Due = total (subtotal) - paid amount.
Paid amount defaults to 0.
The `paidAmount` value is submitted to the backend when creating the invoice.
Fields are styled consistently with the amber/teal design theme.

requirements.text: Update the Invoice Detail page and the printable invoice layout to display the 'Paid Amount' and 'Balance Due' fields. These should appear in the totals/summary section of the invoice detail view and in the printed invoice output generated by the InvoicePrintHeader/printable area. Style consistently with the existing theme; both fields should be visible on-screen and in the printed layout.

quotes: added paid amount and balance amount option

acceptanceCriteria: Invoice Detail page displays a 'Paid Amount' row in the totals section.
Invoice Detail page displays a 'Balance Due' row in the totals section.
Balance Due = total - paid amount.
The printable invoice layout (print/PDF output) includes both Paid Amount and Balance Due rows.
Fields are styled consistently with the amber/teal theme.
The 'Paid Amount' and 'Balance Due' rows are hidden from the printed layout only if the design requires it — otherwise they should appear in print as well.

requirements.text: Update the Invoice List page and Dashboard summary stats to reflect the new paid/balance amounts where relevant. On the Invoice List, optionally show balance due in the table. On the Dashboard, ensure revenue or payment stats are consistent with the introduction of partial payments (paidAmount vs total).

quotes: added paid amount and balance amount option

acceptanceCriteria: Invoice List table shows 'Balance Due' or 'Paid Amount' as a column or within the row, clearly labeled.
Dashboard stats remain accurate and do not break with the new fields.
No undefined or NaN values appear for invoices created before the new fields were added (graceful fallback to 0).

constraints: Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor.
Do not re-introduce any GST/tax fields or calculations.
Balance Due must always be calculated as total minus paidAmount — never stored independently of those two values in the UI.
nonGoals: Partial payment history or payment tracking over time.
Payment method or transaction reference fields.
Any changes to the existing amber/teal visual theme beyond adding the new fields.
